title: After Midnight
stanzas: I breathe through cool breezes,
These moments are all I can lend,
I plead with you with folded hands,
O night, please don't end.
I hate tomorrows and overmorrows still,
Days anew and I'll have to pretend,
I would rather be in your arms and true,
O night, please don't end.
I want this time to be mine tonight,
I want to never ever depend.
But morning comes and resets the world,
O night, please don't end.
Enduring this tiredness just for you,
Without a worry of the ascend,
This dark silence is a great comfort,
O night, please don't end.
Awake for a forever night,
Now that is what I truly intend,
But promise me this when I slumber on,
O night, please don't end.

title: Rain
stanzas: For one to love the cloudy days,
When the weather falls down undone,
It takes a special man, she said
Anyone can love the sun.
So I felt the drops on my face.
First annoyed, and then felt cool,
The sky felt like a luxury,
And I felt like a missing fool.
My mind smiled to see her so,
Smiling and dancing in the rain,
The pour was music enough,
I could live this day forever again.
My wet clothes hugged me tighter,
The chill had her warm smile.
Time had stopped to look at us dance,
And lingered there for a while.
I'd never give it a second look,
But that day I saw it again,
Fell in love with two things then,
First her, and then the rain.

title: Shapely
stanzas: Shapely, shown to a connoisseur,
Shining shade of sublime beauty,
Voluptuous, kind and really smart,
But cannot say "sh", is this cutie.
Laughs a melody too divine,
Fills the world with her smile,
Her voice resonates with the heart,
Inches away, but a billion mile.
Clumsy, but only with my heart,
A koyal who coos into my mind,
Sensual, sexy and near my heart,
Her voice pushes through my rind.
But o beauty you curse this world,
Smashing these eclipses to the floors,
No one could even wish to have,
These beautiful, shiny lips like yours.
The curse you put thus so,
No man can sleep easy now on.
For having seen your lips, your face, your shape,
Can never again be forlorn.
So rejoice, be happy for now the pain,
To oblivion has dwindled and curled.
You make this life a better place,
With the most beautiful smile in the world.

title: Lips
stanzas: It must have been tough for the sculptor's hand,
Destined to craft your lips so grand,
Patience that spanned ten thousand years,
Felt like mere moments, it appears.
Each crevice, a secret to be told,
In the moon's shadow, its story unfolds,
The curves at the edges, they swoon,
Yet I'm forever stuck, a loon.
Through brambles, we must boldly stride,
Worse deeds than death, we'll cast aside,
To find a maiden fair and true,
Kissing her, a braver thing to do.

title: Partings
stanzas: When I see those pink petals,
I long to have a taste,
I would cast them in marble,
Whatever it takes.
They must be warm and home,
I imagined them selfishly again,
And joining with them in eternity,
I would gladly take the pain.
The air is filled with sweetness,
When spirits kiss you then me.
I wish I were the wind again,
So I'd be sweeter than me.
But it is truly the warmth that is,
Dispeller of all the world's cold.
The fact that I could kiss you.
And you in my arms hold.
Immortal would be your marble cast,
But your warmth it could never match,
No one could chisel me away.
Not a glimpse, could anyone catch.
So the chance returned that day,
And I could hold you forever again,
Our lips would touch noisily at once.
But alas alas, what is this pain?
No jest, no honour, no life I see,
You smile but are no longer bold.
All I wanted was just some warmth,
But I find your lips deathly cold.

title: Dare to Ask
stanzas: Let me write a rhyme today,
Some painter has colored me so.
Who dares to love in this world?
So fast it moves, and me so slow.
The world seems fake and faker still,
Who can trust these words again?
Anyone can now fashion poems,
No one knows, through prompt or pain.
These beauties I see around me so,
Are the distractions of a temple wall.
They lure the ego and keep it out,
No man unturned and made a thrall.
But the one who dares to enter,
Sees the world in the womb right there,
We pray to her with joined hands,
She showers on us, her love and care.
Let me explain my affliction,
I do not know where to look.
No one to guide me, through the plane,
No answer found in any book.
I do not know how to look for love,
I do not know what to look for,
I do not know if is it time,
And yet I brave this unknown war.
I do find someone colorful,
And yet I fear if it is even true,
I don't want to impose myself,
But I believe in true love too.
What should I say, to begin the talk?
All I can think of, is her praise,
But empty it feels when I say it to her,
And silence there is for days and days.
Shallow talk is not for me.
I wish to converse for ten thousand years,
To hold her hand, and soul in mine,
Till the end of time appears.
If I must, then I will be alone,
If that is the will of Gods for me,
But I don't think that is the case,
Or I wouldn't be writing this poetry.
So I don't know if it is right,
But I must forgo these temple walls,
And take the steps inside the stones,
Or willingly succumb to these calls.
I'd love to explore the vast cosmos,
Within and without, I guarantee,
All I ask, is for your colorful hands,
Will you, in love, be joining me?

title: Anger
stanzas: Rage, rage, burning fire's breath,
From inferno's eternal depth.
A star ascending with its might,
Destroying all in its fierce flight.
Not the life-giving warmth it brings,
But fear in animal hearts it stings.
Bringing the end, all-consuming fire,
Leaving only ashes in the pyre.
Within its chaos, peace does hide,
Amidst the ruins, purified.
Rebirth awaits in ashes' bed,
Yet death remains, its touch widespread.
Betrayal's fire be truly cold,
Spine's icy grip, a tale untold.
Tears like blood from wounds do fall,
But cruel flames consume all.
Speak you must, a language of fire,
Revenge breeds evil, a cruel mire.
To release the grip, a saint's decree,
I am no saint, I am just me.
Why must this burden unfair persist,
Born from darkness, a relentless twist.
Corrupting all within its range,
A cycle of suffering, a haunting change.
One must endure this pitiful plight.
No one else will fight my fight.
I must eventually get up again.
I must eventually heal my pain.
This woe must not become some fashion.
My strength must be unleashed, unrationed.
These burdens who else will pick?
I must be strong, there is no trick.
This rage will never go away.
A steel cage must stay its sway.
A monster it holds, may all beware.
But I shall reign over it with care.
The heart now grows heavy,
No more poetry to levy.
No meekness, only love resides.
I have so many with me, besides.

title: Winds
stanzas: I am the summer nights breeze
I am the furious blowing wind,
I am the tornadoes that spin,
Staying still is what I didn't.
I fly high into the sky,
To high realms of certain death,
I cool the angers and the metal,
I am the one who fills the breath.
I rush over gurgling waters,
I thin out over the mounts,
I become the true cosmic force,
Propel you to the stars abound.
I push and pull the tides around,
I ravage ships that go across,
Fourty nine ways I can go,
But can't stay still is where I lost.
I carry birds and flying metal,
Bugs to rats and letters too,
I want it all to finally stop,
Atleast O gods, let me be true.
I want it all to finally stop,
I want my movements to have an end.
I want to be just calm and still,
And go back to from, where I was rend.
But through the storms and rains,
I see the noble swift fly,
It has to migrate to distant lands,
To breed or in the process die.
And I find my yearning loosen,
Among the mud the lotus blooms,
So peace I must find on the move,
There are for me no static rooms.
In the rush must I find the peace?
And why not, it is my call.
The cosmos depends on my move,
And I can't let anything fall.

title: Sword
stanzas: Why do we love?
Is it to mate and fill the world?
Or is it to thrill the chase?
Or maybe it is there to soften the world?
Love is not just warm and cozy.
It sometimes dazzles you from the sleep,
It drives you hard and makes you better,
Forces you to do the promises you keep.
But why do we love?
Are we fashioned for it so deeply,
Is it like hunger or thirst or more?
We rummage through the world, and find no one.
No one but many.
More than just feelings, we act on it.
Run to the sky and bring moonstones
I wish this mystery wasn't so.
So I'd feel less stupid in my actions.
No action done for love goes unpunished.
Kisses or slaps or sometimes others.
Why do we love though so intensely?
And how do we keep doing that forever?
I think it takes some bravery to love.
Love truly that is without shades.
So when you love, and truly love,
You shine like a million suns, unleashed and unlimited.
You are capable of that love, remember you are fashioned for it.
Even loving the wrong things made you a God of it.
What heavens shall you reach if you love the right one!
But what is the right one, who can tell?
So just love everyone and listen to the echo.
You might be loved back so divinely.
It will deafening, but still melodious.
So maybe that is why we love.
To brave the world and go forth.
To love yourself so lovingly.
And to see yourself in everyone.
But be careful it might sting you back.
The world is under no obligations.
But the dragon Slayer afraid of fire, is ignoble.
Sharpen your sword and sheath it, with love.

title: Lune
stanzas: In night's embrace the moon now hovers,
Filling hearts with longing, like lost lovers.
He roams the sky unleashed, a cosmic whim,
And yet husband of the stars who never dim.
He knows no rules, this lunar sprite,
Sometimes full, sometimes out of sight.
Like milk, he's white, his usual hue.
Yet honeyed, blue, and red he'll strew.
No sun's companion, he follows his tune,
Cycles his own, like a mystic rune.
The sun makes him crescent too soon,
He too devours the sun, a true lune.
Beneath his silver lovers find their boon,
In his presence, beneath the mystic moon.
Whispers of love beneath his gentle light,
He watches, the witness of passion's flight.
Tides and winds at his command,
Earth's living body, shaped by his hand.
Born from the churning of his mother's art,
He cools death's sting, a soothing part.
In prayer, we join, and the moon must shine,
Who prays to Lord Shiva from his own shrine,
We pray, to join him in our shallow breath,
The Lord of Dance, and the Lord of Death.

title: Search for Truth
stanzas: Question has been struck now,
Across the time, space and minds,
Across feelings and expressions,
And across all the rinds.
It does not have a word that stays,
No tongue that does not fray or fail,
No map, no path, no borrowed bridge,
By which the seeker might set sail.
Is the search for Truth, O fire, the subtlest form of Maya?
There is no search for the Omni,
The Guru says with a quiet smile.
And who insists that Maya is false?
That creed is almost juvenile.
Student asks for description,
How do we then see the Truth?
Silence was the answer given,
Who is old here, who is youth?
Silence has no border either,
No shape, no color, and no size.
And that is the truth that we must,
Surmise then forget, then realize.
There is nothing to do but Witness,
There is no doer in this void,
It's all properties just conversing,
And You are false to think you toiled.
Do not look for an answer here,
This is not dual, no fire or ice,
Words, language or even your mind,
Nothing covers the silence, nothing shall suffice.
This question is the last refuge,
For then the mind rejects all violence,
As for the one who has truly seen,
He disappears and becomes the Silence.

title: Command of Death
stanzas: My lord asks that I tap on heads
All those who are about to die,
They wait and wait and wait for me,
They plead looking up at the sky.
I bear no face and all the faces,
I hide nothing and come hidden,
I may not come even after decades,
Or come too soon unbidden.
My touch releases these mortal coils,
Some call me death, time or fate.
I rush to wars, but also to hearths,
You find me in wombs or out in space.
I hold no emotion against the crying.
I must do what I must do.
If I falter to some mother's tears.
I will no longer be blessed, be true.
So I resolve with iron breath,
Carefully before my tears freeze,
Not just to touch the tyrant's life,
But part fair maidens, swans, and fleas.
But one I see beyond this world,
How can something so good be gone.
I must touch her, even in prayer.
For more than most, even I must mourn.
Some so bright, the sun sets instantly,
Them when they go even the gods miss,
For them I don't lay my wicked fingers,
For them on their lips, I gently kiss.
